title: Storage format is JSONL only
content: Memory source of truth is .ctxmem/memory.jsonl (append-only, committed to git). index.db is a derived SQLite FTS5 index, git-ignored and rebuilt with 'ctxmem sync'. Never edit or commit index.db.

title: Keyword search is the stable default
content: Default search mode is keyword (SQLite FTS5, zero dependencies). semantic and hybrid modes (Ollama + sqlite-vec) are BETA/experimental and under active testing; recall falls back to keyword if the backend is unavailable.

title: Zero required core dependencies
content: The core has no third-party dependencies. Optional extras: mcp, semantic, bench. Requires Python 3.8+ with SQLite FTS5 support.

title: Git Flow branching model
content: Use Git Flow: main (released code) and develop (integration). feature/* and bugfix/* branch from and merge into develop; hotfix/* branches from main and merges into both main and develop. See CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: Memory is shared via git
content: Root .gitignore ignores only .ctxmem/index.db so that .ctxmem/memory.jsonl and .ctxmem/config.json are committed. Contributors pull them and get the same project context; the index rebuilds locally with 'ctxmem sync'.

title: Project layout
content: CLI: src/ctxmem/cli.py. Storage: store.py. Retrieval/search: retrieval.py. MCP server: mcp_server.py. Embeddings (beta): embeddings.py. Code indexer: indexer.py. Benchmark: bench.py. Git helpers: gitinfo.py.

title: MCP server integration
content: Console script ctxmem-mcp exposes recall/remember/memory_status tools to AI agents. Wire it up with 'ctxmem agent-init --mcp' which writes .vscode/mcp.json. Agent instructions live in .github/copilot-instructions.md (and AGENTS.md for Codex).

title: Dev setup, tests and release
content: Dev install: pip install -e ".[all]". No automated test suite yet; validate changes by running the CLI (init -> remember -> sync -> recall). Release: bump version in pyproject.toml and src/ctxmem/__init__.py, PR develop->main, tag vX.Y.Z (SemVer).

title: Repository and license
content: Repository: https://github.com/DoppiaG93/ctxmem. License: MIT. Current version lives in pyproject.toml (single source of truth); latest release line is 1.3.x.

title: README demo is an animated SVG
content: The README demo is an animated SVG at assets/demo.svg, generated with termtosvg. The generation scripts (scripts/demo.sh, scripts/record-demo.sh) are maintainer-only: kept local and gitignored (scripts/), NOT shipped in the repo. Regenerate with 'bash scripts/record-demo.sh'.

title: Demo recall must match in keyword mode
content: The demo's seeded memory intentionally contains the word 'authentication' so 'ctxmem recall "how do we handle authentication"' returns a real hit in the default keyword mode (FTS5 has no stemming, so query words must appear literally).

title: GitHub community files are force-tracked
content: Root .gitignore ignores .github/* by design (keeps copilot-instructions.md and AGENTS.md local). Community health files are re-included via explicit negations: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, dependabot.yml, and workflows/.

title: CI and community policy files
content: CI via GitHub Actions: test.yml (pytest on Python 3.8/3.11/3.12 + CLI smoke) and lint.yml (pylint on src/ctxmem and tests); README shows both badges. Policies: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant 2.1) and SECURITY.md (private vulnerability reporting via GitHub advisories).

title: Current version is 1.3.x
content: The project baseline version is 1.3.0. This security/contribution documentation fix is released as the 1.3.1 patch. Keep pyproject.toml and src/ctxmem/__init__.py in sync with the released version; SECURITY.md supports the 1.3.x line.

title: Contributions are invite-only
content: External contributions are currently restricted: only invited collaborators may open pull requests. Bug reports and feature requests via GitHub issues remain open to everyone. This is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md and the README Contributing section.

title: Version single source of truth
content: pyproject.toml is the single source of truth for the version. src/ctxmem/__init__.py derives __version__ via importlib.metadata.version('ctxmem'), falling back to 0.0.0 when not installed. After bumping pyproject.toml, reinstall the editable package (pip install -e .) so the metadata refreshes.

title: Supersede + mandatory recall/reconcile protocol
content: Added self-interrogation loop: recall now demotes and flags superseded memories (⚠ SUPERSEDED / ↳ replaces). New 'supersedes' field on records (store schema col + supersede_index()), 'remember --supersedes <id>' in CLI and MCP (prints new id), mem_id carried through the semantic/embeddings path, and retrieval.search annotates+stable-sorts stale records to the bottom. AGENT_PROTOCOL rewritten to be mandatory: recall before every answer, reconcile code-vs-memory conflicts, remember/supersede corrections.

title: ask verdict + staleness detection
content: Added 'ctxmem ask' (CLI + MCP tool) that runs recall and prefixes a verdict: HIT (>=1 active non-superseded memory record), WEAK (only code symbols or superseded notes), MISS (nothing). Verdict logic in cli._verdict/mcp _verdict. Added staleness detection in retrieval._annotate: a memory record whose 'path' points to a file missing on disk is flagged '_stale' and shown as '⚠ STALE (missing file: <path>)' in recall/ask (code changed => verify/supersede). Renamed _annotate_supersede -> _annotate(conn, rows, root). Refactored shared recall/ask argparse into _add_search_args. AGENT_PROTOCOL now tells agents to run 'ctxmem ask' first.
